text.title: langchain-workflows Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 high-impact failure modes for langchain-workflows with detectors and fixes that map back to rules in 01-core-rules.xml.

antipattern.symptom: Checkpoint deserialise fails after schema bump
antipattern.root-cause: WorkflowState dict was deeply nested; nested dict added a field, old checkpoints can't load.
antipattern.fix: Keep state flat; bump a schema_version field and write a migration; validate on load.
antipattern.detector: Reload an old checkpoint after refactor; if KeyError, you've broken the contract.

antipattern.symptom: Human approval bypassed because someone added a 'pre-flight' node before the interrupt
antipattern.root-cause: interrupt_before was set on stale node name; refactor moved the destructive node.
antipattern.fix: Tag destructive nodes with a decorator; CI greps for @destructive and asserts each is in interrupt_before.
antipattern.detector: Lint pass: every @destructive must be in compile(interrupt_before=[...]).

antipattern.symptom: `messages` accumulator hits 50MB and checkpointing slows to a crawl
antipattern.fix: Insert compaction node every N turns: summarise and reset.
antipattern.detector: Track checkpoint size metric; alert at 5MB.

antipattern.symptom: Workflow can't resume after pod restart
antipattern.fix: Use SqliteSaver for single-instance or PostgresSaver for multi-instance; never MemorySaver outside tests.
antipattern.detector: Kill the process mid-workflow; verify resume from new pod.
